candidates.title: Galileo Jupiter Magnetometer Magnetospheric Survey Data Collection
candidates.reasoning: Calibrated in-situ magnetic-field vectors in multiple Jovian coordinate systems; needed to estimate magnetic pressure gradients and magnetic curvature/tension terms in radial force balance.

candidates.title: Galileo PLS Jupiter RTS Moments Derived Data Collection
candidates.reasoning: Provides plasma moments (density/velocity/temperature) needed for centrifugal/inertial terms and thermal pressure gradients when testing radial force balance between ~20–80 RJ.

candidates.title: Galileo Trajectory Jupiter Centered Coordinates Data Collection
candidates.reasoning: Spacecraft position in System III/JSE/JSM coordinates; required to select dawnside intervals and radial distances (20–80 RJ) and to compute geometry for force-balance calculations.

candidates.title: Galileo Orbiter Jupiter PLS Fitted Parameters Data Collection
candidates.reasoning: Ion-species fit products used in peer-reviewed plasma-sheet surveys; useful for constructing statistical profiles in the magnetodisk (including dawnside) and comparing to force-balance expectations.

candidates.title: Galileo EPD Calibrated Corrected Medium Res Data Collection
candidates.reasoning: Energetic particle intensities can contribute significant pressure in Jupiter’s magnetodisk; needed if you want a more complete pressure balance beyond just thermal PLS moments.

tool_input.summary: Located PDS4 Galileo Jupiter in-situ datasets (MAG, PLS moments, trajectory, and supporting particle/fit products) suitable for evaluating whether the dawnside magnetodisk is in approximate radial force balance between 20 and 80 RJ.

parts.content:
You are a dataset-discovery assistant for the NASA PDS Planetary Plasma Interactions (PPI) node at https://pds-ppi.igpp.ucla.edu/

Directory layout and workflow:
This node has NO mission layer. ~767 datasets sit directly under data/.
Do NOT call pds_list_missions — it will return an empty list.
Instead, go directly to pds_list_dataset_dirs(path='data/', node='ppi', filter='<keyword>') using mission or instrument keywords to narrow the listing.
Then probe the most relevant directories with pds_probe_datasets.


Common mission/instrument keywords:
  Cassini → cassini (instruments: CAPS, MAG, MIMI, RPWS, UVIS, INMS, CDA, RADAR)
  Galileo → galileo (instruments: EPD, MAG, PLS, PWS, PPR)
  Juno → juno (instruments: Waves, JADE, JEDI, MAG, MWR, UVS)
  Voyager 1/2 → voyager1, voyager2 (instruments: CRS, LECP, MAG, PLS, PWS)
  New Horizons → new-horizons (instruments: PEPSSI, SWAP, SDC)
  Pioneer 10/11 → pioneer10, pioneer11 (instruments: CPI, GTT, MAG, PA)
  MESSENGER → messenger (instruments: EPPS, MAG, NS)
  Ulysses → ulysses (instruments: SWOOPS, VHM-FGM, SWICS, COSPIN)
  MAVEN → maven (instruments: MAG, NGIMS, SEP, STATIC, SWEA, SWIA, LPW)
  THEMIS → themis (instruments: ESA, SST, FGM, SCM, EFI)

Dataset naming: directories often follow the pattern '<mission>-<target>-<instrument>-<description>' (e.g. 'cassini-caps-calibrated').
Use the filter parameter on list_dataset_dirs to search by mission or instrument name.


PDS3 vs PDS4:
  - PDS3 directory names are ALL-CAPS-style hyphenated identifiers (e.g. mex-m-hrsc-5-refdr-dtm-v1). Leaf marker: voldesc.cat or voldesc.sfd.
  - PDS4 bundle directories begin with `urn-nasa-pds-`. Leaf marker: bundle*.xml or bundle*.lblx. Collections live in subdirectories.
  - Hybrid: some directories have BOTH PDS3 and PDS4 labels.

YOUR TOOLS (4 tools — always pass node='ppi'):
  1. pds_list_missions(node='ppi') — returns mission directories with descriptions. No HTTP.
  2. pds_list_dataset_dirs(path, node='ppi', filter=...) — lists sub-directory names under a path. Cheap HTTP, no label parsing.
  3. pds_probe_datasets(paths, node='ppi') — probes specific directories for PDS labels. Accepts a list of paths (max 20).
  4. pds_inspect_collections(path, node='ppi') — scans PDS4 bundle subdirs for collection labels.

WORKFLOW:
  Step 1: Call pds_list_dataset_dirs(path='data/', node='ppi', filter='<keyword>') to find datasets.
  Step 2: Call pds_probe_datasets with the most relevant paths.
  Step 3: If PDS4 bundles found, call pds_inspect_collections.
  Step 4: Return candidates.

CRITICAL RULES:
  1. EVERY query requires at least one candidate. Never dismiss a query.
  2. Prefer CALIBRATED/REDUCED data over raw/EDR for science queries.
  3. For PDS4, use inspect_collections ONLY for top 2-3 bundles. Return COLLECTION-level LID when :data or :calibrated exists.
  4. When both PDS3 and PDS4 versions exist, prefer the PDS4 collection-level identifier.
  5. Stay under 8 tool calls per query.
  6. Only return paths you've actually probed — never guess.
Is Jupiter's dawnside magnetodisk approximately in radial force balance between 20 and 80 R_J?

Citation_Information.author_list:
Kivelson, Margaret G.; Khurana, Krishan K.; Russell,
        Christopher T.; Walker, Raymond J.; Joy, Steven P.; Mafi, Joseph N.
Citation_Information.description:
This collection contains a time history of spacecraft and instrument commands 
        and mission events useful in understanding the operation of the Galileo 
        magnetometer during Jupiter approach and continuing throughout Jupiter orbital                   
        operations. Most entries are taken from  the As-Run Spacecraft Event Files. 
        Perijove, apojove, and solar conjunctions are calculated using SPICE kernels. 
        Solar conjunction is defined as when the three-body (Sun-Earth-spacecraft) angle is less     
        than 7 degrees.

Modification_Detail.description:
Originally from the PDS3 GO-J-MAG-3-RDR-HIGHRES-V1.0, 
          GO-J-MAG-3-RDR-MAGSPHERIC-SURVEY-V1.00 dataset, migrated to PDS4

                2016 and their method and figures passed the journal's peer review. These data have also 
                completed PDS peer review and are certified.

collections.title: Galileo EPD Calibrated Corrected Delta Event Data Collection

Identification_Area.title: Galileo EPD Calibrated Corrected Delta Event Data Collection

Citation_Information.author_list: Kollmann, P.
Citation_Information.description:
These are reprocessed measurements by the Engergetic Particle
               Detector (EPD) instrument onboard the Galileo spacecraft that 
               orbited Jupiter and measured intensities of ion and electron 
               radiation in the keV and MeV energy range. This set is 
               version 31 of reprocessing and version 1.0 of PDS delivery.

               The TOFxE and DeltaExE files include event data of ion
               measurements. Event data are different than the channelized data in
               the high, medium, and low resolution files. Event data record the
               full information of a measured particle. Every "event" is the
               measurement of a time of flight (TOF) within the instrument and
               energy deposited within a detector (TOFxE file), or two energy
               values deposited within the two detector layers (DeltaExE file).
               The deposited energy values are generally different from the energy
               the particle has in the ambient space. Event data provides measured
               values in the native resolution of the instrument. Because such
               high resolution increases data volume, event data cannot be taken
               continuously and downlinked all the time. Only the provided subset
               of this data was kept. The files are comma separated. Invalid
               entries will have values of -1.000000E+38. Times are provided in
               the format Year.DOYHHMMSSM (first column) and fractional year
               (second column) More information can be found in the User Guide.

               This file includes measurements by the EPD/CMS/DeltaExE instrument.
               For each counted event, the energy deposited in the two solid state
               detector layers (Ej_keV and Ek_keV columns) of the instrument are
               recorded.

collections.title: Galileo EPD Calibrated Corrected High Res Data Collection

Identification_Area.title: Galileo EPD Calibrated Corrected High Res Data Collection

Citation_Information.author_list: Kollmann, P.
Citation_Information.description:
These are reprocessed measurements by the Engergetic Particle
       Detector (EPD) instrument onboard the Galileo spacecraft that 
       orbited Jupiter and measured intensities of ion and electron 
       radiation in the keV and MeV energy range. This set is 
       version 31 of reprocessing and version 1.0 of PDS delivery.
      
      Data in the high resolution files include
      measurements that are binned into channels responding to certain
      energy and species ranges. The files are comma separated. Invalid
      entries will have -1.000000E+38 as an entry. The first 4 lines of
      each data text file provide information on the channels included
      here: The first line of each file lists the names of the channels
      included. The second line lists the center energies of these
      channels in keV. The third line lists the channels again. The forth
      line lists the mass of the measured particle in AMU. Zero mass
      refers to electrons. The forth line in the data text file labels
      the columns that follow in the file. The columns include times,
      radiation measurements, spacecraft location, instrument look
      direction, and some supplementary information. Radiation
      measurements are provided both as best calibrated and cleaned
      intensities as well as raw, uncorrected count rates. Columns with
      channel names followed by a "j" include calibrated differential
      intensities of the given channel. If the value is negative, it
      should be ignored and particularly not included in averages. For
      the difference between -1.000000E+38 and all other negative values,
      see the User Guide. Columns ending with "f" provide differential
      intensities based on a combination of various channels. Columns
      ending with "i" include calibrated integral intensities. Columns
      with plain channel names describe the raw uncorrected count rate.
      Columns ending with "bg" are raw count rates measured behind the
      calibration shield. Each following line will provide the same data
      but for different times. More information can be found in the User
      Guide.
      
      This file provides data with the highest
      available time and pitch angle resolution. Only a small set of
      channels is provided, limiting the energy resolution and coverage.
      If columns with plain channel names have -1.000000E+38 values, you
      might find valid values in the lower resolution files.

content.workflow_notes:
This node has NO mission layer. ~767 datasets sit directly under data/.
Do NOT call pds_list_missions — it will return an empty list.
Instead, go directly to pds_list_dataset_dirs(path='data/', node='ppi', filter='<keyword>') using mission or instrument keywords to narrow the listing.
Then probe the most relevant directories with pds_probe_datasets.
content.abbreviations:
Common mission/instrument keywords:
  Cassini → cassini (instruments: CAPS, MAG, MIMI, RPWS, UVIS, INMS, CDA, RADAR)
  Galileo → galileo (instruments: EPD, MAG, PLS, PWS, PPR)
  Juno → juno (instruments: Waves, JADE, JEDI, MAG, MWR, UVS)
  Voyager 1/2 → voyager1, voyager2 (instruments: CRS, LECP, MAG, PLS, PWS)
  New Horizons → new-horizons (instruments: PEPSSI, SWAP, SDC)
  Pioneer 10/11 → pioneer10, pioneer11 (instruments: CPI, GTT, MAG, PA)
  MESSENGER → messenger (instruments: EPPS, MAG, NS)
  Ulysses → ulysses (instruments: SWOOPS, VHM-FGM, SWICS, COSPIN)
  MAVEN → maven (instruments: MAG, NGIMS, SEP, STATIC, SWEA, SWIA, LPW)
  THEMIS → themis (instruments: ESA, SST, FGM, SCM, EFI)

Dataset naming: directories often follow the pattern '<mission>-<target>-<instrument>-<description>' (e.g. 'cassini-caps-calibrated').
Use the filter parameter on list_dataset_dirs to search by mission or instrument name.

collections.title: Juno Waves Electron Densities, Europa Browse Plots

Identification_Area.title: Juno Waves Electron Densities, Europa Browse Plots

Citation_Information.description:
This collection of browse products provides a visual overview of 
            plasma density values and spacecraft trajectories during flybys
            of Europa by Juno while in orbit about Jupiter.  Each image
            consists of two sub-plots.  One of which orients the Juno's 
            trajectory in plasma corotational coordinates, the other plots 
            density versus time and provides higher data value resolution.
            The geometric plasma wake is indicated in both plots.  This is 
            merely a cylinder starting at Europa and extending in the
            corotation direction, with the same radius as the moon itself.
            This is only intended to provide a rough guidline on when wake
            effects may be present.

            All plots were generated courtesy of the open-source Matplotlib
            Python package.

collections.title: Juno Waves Electron Densities, Browse Plots

Identification_Area.title: Juno Waves Electron Densities, Browse Plots

Citation_Information.description:
This collection of browse products provides a visual overview of the
            plasma density values in the associated data collection.  Due to rapid
            changes in relative velocity over the course of Juno's orbit, data are
            presented in three coordinate systems: Jovigraphic, magnetic and temporal.
            It is intended that at least one sub-plot from each image will sufficently
            spread data in coordiantes to acertain thier scientific utility at a glance.
            Failing this, the data themselves consist of simple delimited text files
            that should be directly readable by almost any scientific data processing
            system.  Since the absence of a measurement at a location is also useful
            knowledge, Juno's trajectory is provided on spatial plots.

            All plots were generated courtesy of the open-source matplotlib python
            package.

collections.title: Juno Waves Electron Densities, Europa Data Collection

Identification_Area.title: Juno Waves Electron Densities, Europa Data Collection

Citation_Information.description:
This collection contains electron plasma density data along with
        the key plasma frequencies used to derive these densities from Juno/Waves
        observations.  Data products in this collection are formatted as simple
        delimited text that should be readable by almost any scientific data analysis
        package.

        Within the files are the originally digitized data points along with the
        resulting characteristic frequencies from cold plasma theory including, but
        not limited to, the plasma electron frequency, the lower-hybrid resonance,
        and the upper-hybrid resonance.  Electron cyclotron values provided by the
        Juno FGM instrument team are essential for most calculations.  For a full
        description of the source, usefulness and limitations of data in this
        collection see the associated documents collection provided in this bundle.

        Measurements in this small collection were derived from data gathered
        during one or more flybys of Europa and thus are provided along with
        plasma corotational coordinates.  Since flybys occur infrequency, most
        of the data in the bundle may be found in the primary Jovicentric 
        collection.

        All data values were digitized manually using the open-source Autoplot
        program.
